judgmentZh: 中孚：豚鱼吉，利涉大川，利贞。
judgmentEn: Inner Truth. Pigs and fishes. Good fortune. It furthers one to cross the great water. Perseverance furthers.
imageZh: 泽上有风，中孚；君子以议狱缓死。
imageEn: Wind over lake: the image of Inner Truth. Thus the superior man discusses criminal cases in order to delay executions.
interpretationZh: 中孚卦象征内心的诚信，风吹泽上，感化万物。以至诚之心待人，即使猪鱼之微也能感通，利于涉险。
interpretationEn: Zhong Fu symbolizes inner sincerity. Wind blowing over the lake penetrates all things. With utmost sincerity, even the lowliest creatures are moved.

lines.textZh: 初九：虞吉，有它不燕。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: Being prepared brings good fortune. If there are secret designs, it is disquieting.
lines.interpretationZh: 有所准备则吉利，若心有旁骛则不安宁。
lines.interpretationEn: Preparedness brings fortune. Hidden agendas bring unease.
lines.detailZh: 初九曰“虞吉，有它不燕”。处卦之始，诚信刚刚建立，最重要的是专一守信，不受旁念干扰。“虞”有安备、审慎之意，说明起初若能怀着警觉而不轻忽，则可得吉。若心中又生他念，或对关系、承诺、方向三心二意，就会失去安宁。“不燕”即不得安宴。此爻告诫人们，在信任刚萌芽时，切忌分心与试探，应以谨慎和专注培育其根基。
lines.detailEn: The first line says, “Prepared confidence brings good fortune. If there are other aims, there is no peace.” At the beginning of Inner Truth, trust is only starting to form, so the key is focused sincerity. “Prepared” suggests alertness and careful grounding rather than naïve openness. Good fortune comes when one is inwardly settled and clear in intention. But if other motives enter—divided loyalties, hidden agendas, or emotional wavering—peace is lost. This line teaches that early trust must be protected by concentration and purity of purpose.

lines.textZh: 九二：鸣鹤在阴，其子和之。我有好爵，吾与尔靡之。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: A crane calling in the shade. Its young answers it. I have a good goblet. I will share it with you.
lines.interpretationZh: 鹤在树荫中鸣叫，幼鹤应声相和。诚信之感应，如同分享美酒。
lines.interpretationEn: A crane calls from the shade and its young responds. Sincerity resonates like sharing a fine goblet.
lines.detailZh: 九二曰“鹤鸣在阴，其子和之。我有好爵，吾与尔靡之。”此爻居中得正，是中孚全卦最美的一爻。鹤在幽静处鸣叫，小鹤自然应和，象征真诚的声音虽不张扬，却能引发自然共鸣。后句言有美酒佳爵，愿与人共享，表示诚信不仅能沟通心灵，还能促成分享与同乐。此爻主内诚外应，说明只要发于真心，自会有人响应，无须强求。
lines.detailEn: The second line says, “A crane calls in the shade; its young answers it. I have a fine goblet; I will share it with you.” This is the most harmonious line of the hexagram, centered and balanced. The crane’s call in a hidden place evokes sincerity that is quiet yet deeply resonant. The young bird answers naturally, showing that true feeling evokes response without force. The image of sharing a fine cup adds the idea of trust creating fellowship, generosity, and mutual delight. This line teaches that genuine inner truth naturally finds its echo.

lines.textZh: 六三：得敌，或鼓或罢，或泣或歌。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: He finds a comrade. Now he beats the drum, now he stops. Now he sobs, now he sings.
lines.interpretationZh: 遇到对手，时而击鼓进攻，时而停止；时而哭泣，时而歌唱，情绪不定。
lines.interpretationEn: Finding a match, one alternates between drumming and stopping, weeping and singing. Emotions are unstable.
lines.detailZh: 六三曰“得敌，或鼓或罢，或泣或歌。”此爻阴柔失中，处内外交界，最易情绪动荡。所谓“得敌”，是遭遇对立、竞争或内心冲突，故而一时振作击鼓，一时疲惫停歇；一时悲泣，一时高歌，反复无常。中孚之道贵稳定真实，而此爻恰恰显示信念未定、情感摇摆的状态。其警示在于：若内心不真不定，就容易被外界牵着走，难以保持持续的信任与判断。
lines.detailEn: The third line says, “He meets an opponent. Now he beats the drum, now he stops; now he weeps, now he sings.” This line is unstable, standing at a threshold and easily agitated. The ‘opponent’ may be an external rival or an internal conflict. The alternating actions—drumming and stopping, weeping and singing—show emotional volatility and inconsistency. Since Inner Truth depends on steady authenticity, this line reveals what happens when one lacks inner composure: moods swing, judgment falters, and trust becomes unreliable.

lines.textZh: 六四：月几望，马匹亡，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The moon nearly at the full. The team horse goes astray. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 月亮将满，马匹走失，但无过失。接近圆满时宜舍弃旧伴。
lines.interpretationEn: The moon is nearly full. A horse strays from its team. No blame in letting go.
lines.detailZh: 六四曰“月几望，马匹亡，无咎。”月近圆而未全圆，象征德行接近成熟，但仍需克制，不可自满。马匹亡，可理解为舍弃外在驱驰争逐之具，或失去依恃却因此免咎。处近君之位，若能不争功、不恃势，反而得安。此爻说明中孚发展到一定阶段时，要懂得留白与止盈，宁可减去一些外在助力，也不要因过满而招损。
lines.detailEn: The fourth line says, “The moon is nearly full. The horses are lost. No blame.” A moon almost full suggests a condition nearing completion but not yet overflowing. This is a moment requiring restraint rather than self-satisfaction. The loss of horses may symbolize giving up outward means of striving, speed, or dependence. Though something useful is gone, there is no blame because the loss prevents excess. This line teaches that as trust matures, one must avoid overreaching. Modesty and release preserve what ambition might damage.

lines.textZh: 九五：有孚挛如，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: He possesses truth, which links together. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 以诚信紧密联结众人，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Sincerity binds all together. No blame.
lines.detailZh: 九五曰“有孚挛如，无咎。”九五为尊位，居中得正，是以诚信系天下之象。“挛如”有联结、牵系之义，表示以真实信用把人心维系起来，不靠威压而靠德信。处领导位置者，若能言行一致、公正可靠，便可使群体自愿归附，无所咎害。这一爻是中孚之极致，说明最高层次的领导与关系经营，根本都在一个“信”字。
lines.detailEn: The fifth line says, “There is truth that binds together. No blame.” In the ruling place, centered and correct, this line shows the fullest expression of Inner Truth. “Binds together” means that sincerity creates cohesion. People are held not by fear or manipulation but by trust in the integrity of the center. For a leader, this means consistency, fairness, and moral reliability. When truth holds a group together in this way, nothing is blameworthy. This is the political and relational peak of the hexagram.

lines.textZh: 上九：翰音登于天，贞凶。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: Cockcrow penetrating to heaven. Perseverance brings misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 鸡鸣之声传至天际，虚声张扬，坚持则凶。
lines.interpretationEn: A cockcrow reaches heaven — empty noise. Persisting in pretense brings misfortune.
lines.detailZh: 上九曰“翰音登于天，贞凶。”翰音高飞入天，象征声名、言辞、理想上扬到过度拔高的地步。中孚本贵内实，此爻却有外声过盛、名高于实之象。即使自以为坚持不变，仍可能因脱离实际而致凶。“贞凶”说明这里的问题不是不坚持，而是坚持错了方向，把诚信变成了空洞口号。此爻警告：真正的中孚在于内在笃实，不在于把声音传得多高。
lines.detailEn: The top line says, “The soaring sound rises to heaven. Perseverance brings misfortune.” Here sound ascends too high, suggesting reputation, declarations, or ideals lifted beyond reality. Inner Truth values substance, but this line shows what happens when outward expression exceeds inward grounding. One may remain persistent, yet still meet misfortune because the persistence is attached to emptiness, display, or self-inflation. The warning is clear: sincerity must remain embodied and real. Once it turns into lofty rhetoric without living truth, it loses its power.

overviewZh: 中孚卦，上巽下兑，风行泽上，外柔而内悦，象征真诚由内而发，并以温和的方式感通外界。所谓“中孚”，不是表面的信誓旦旦，而是内心真实、意念纯正、言行一致，因此能够感动物类，故卦辞言“豚鱼吉”。此卦强调诚信是人与人、人与事、人与天地相通的根本力量。处于中孚之时，最忌伪饰、浮夸、虚声；最贵笃实、守正、持中。它提醒人们，真正的影响力不是强制得来，而是凭借可信与诚意自然形成。无论涉险渡难，还是处理纷争、维系关系，都应先正其心，再正其行。心中有信，则外在局势虽复杂，仍能渐归和顺。
overviewEn: Hexagram 61, Inner Truth, is formed by Xun above and Dui below: wind over the lake. It suggests a subtle movement above a receptive, joyful openness below, symbolizing sincerity that arises from within and gently resonates outward. “Inner Truth” is not loud declaration or moral performance, but authentic alignment between heart, speech, and action. Because of that integrity, even humble beings such as “pigs and fish” are moved, which is why the Judgment says they bring good fortune. This hexagram teaches that trust is a real force. It can cross danger, soften conflict, and create cooperation without coercion. The danger in this time lies in display without substance, emotional instability, or empty rhetoric. The power lies in steadiness, transparency, and moral centeredness. When one is inwardly true, influence becomes natural rather than forced. In personal life, leadership, relationships, and difficult decisions, Inner Truth calls for honest motives, restrained expression, and fidelity to what is right. Through that, even uncertain situations can gradually become workable and harmonious.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞曰：“中孚：豚鱼吉，利涉大川，利贞。”其义在于诚信之感通，足以达于微贱之物，故连豚鱼都可受其德而呈吉象。《彖传》说：“中孚，柔在内而刚得中。说而巽，孚，乃化邦也。豚鱼吉，信及豚鱼也。利涉大川，乘木舟虚也。中孚以利贞，乃应乎天也。”这里指出，中孚之所以成立，在于内里柔和而能容，外有刚中而不偏，再加上兑之悦、巽之顺，于是诚信不仅是个人品德，更能教化人群、安定邦国。所谓“利涉大川”，不是鲁莽冒险，而是在诚信基础上，众人愿意同心协力，故可渡险。最后“利贞”尤为关键：真诚若不合正道，便可能流于感情用事或讨好取信。故中孚之道，必须以守正为根，以真实为本，方能长久无失。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says: “Inner Truth. Good fortune with pigs and fish. It is favorable to cross the great river. It is favorable to be correct and steadfast.” The meaning is that genuine sincerity reaches even the simplest creatures, so “pigs and fish” symbolize the broad, unquestioning power of trust. The Tuan Commentary explains: “In Inner Truth, the yielding are within and the firm attain the center. Joyful and gentle: this is trust, and thus it transforms the state. ‘Pigs and fish, good fortune’ means trust reaches even pigs and fish. ‘Favorable to cross the great river’ means one rides a hollowed wooden boat. ‘Inner Truth is favorable in correctness’ means it accords with Heaven.” This commentary shows that Inner Truth is not sentimentality. It rests on a structure: inward softness, outward centered firmness, joyful openness, and gentle conduct. Such sincerity becomes socially transformative, not merely personal. Crossing the great river symbolizes undertaking a serious difficulty, made possible because trust creates support and shared commitment. Yet the phrase “favorable in correctness” is essential. Sincerity without principle can become naïve emotionalism, manipulation, or self-exposure without wisdom. True Inner Truth must be rooted in uprightness. Only then does trust harmonize human relations with a larger moral order.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“泽上有风，中孚；君子以议狱缓死。”泽本能容，风本能入，风行泽上，形成无形而普遍的感应之势，这正如真诚不靠压迫，而靠渗透与感化。君子观此卦象，明白处理人事不可只凭表面辞色，而要体察情实、审慎求中。因此在审狱断刑时，要反复斟酌，慎用极刑，“缓死”不是纵恶，而是体现对生命、事实与人心的敬重。中孚之象所强调的，是一种带有温度的公正：既不偏私，也不苛急；既讲原则，也重体恤。风泽相感，说明真正的治理与沟通，来自能够进入人心的诚信与体察。
imageDetailEn: The Image says: “Wind above the lake: Inner Truth. The noble one, in accordance with this, discusses legal cases and delays executions.” The lake is open and receptive; the wind enters and moves across it invisibly. Together they portray subtle influence, the kind that works not by force but by penetration and resonance. This is the image of sincerity: quiet, pervasive, and effective. The noble person learns from this that human affairs must not be judged merely from appearances or rigid formulas. One must listen deeply, examine motives, and preserve humane caution. Thus in legal matters the superior person is careful, deliberative, and reluctant to impose irreversible punishment. “Delaying death” does not mean indulgence toward wrongdoing; it means respecting truth, complexity, and human life. The image therefore joins justice with compassion. Inner Truth is not weak softness, but morally informed sensitivity. It teaches that the best governance, judgment, and communication arise from credibility, empathy, and careful discernment rather than haste or harshness.
modernApplicationZh: 事业方面，中孚卦强调信誉先于技巧。与团队、客户或合作方相处时，最重要的不是一时的话术，而是长期稳定的兑现能力。遇到重大项目、转型或跨界合作，可以推进，但前提是目标透明、分工清晰、承诺可落实。感情方面，此卦主以诚感人，适合修复误会、坦白心意、重建信任；但忌讳口头承诺过多而行动跟不上，否则反伤关系。财运方面，中孚不主投机暴利，更利于凭口碑、信用、稳定合作获得收益，投资宜选择自己真正理解且长期看好的方向，避免被虚假包装诱导。健康方面，此卦提醒情绪与身体关联密切，内心压抑、言不由衷容易形成睡眠、胸闷、消化与神经紧张问题；宜通过真诚表达、规律作息、舒缓运动来恢复内外一致。总体而言，中孚在现代生活中的核心启示，是把“可信”当作最重要的资产。
modernApplicationEn: In career matters, Inner Truth teaches that credibility matters more than cleverness. When dealing with teams, clients, or partners, persuasive language is less important than consistent follow-through. Major projects, transitions, or cross-sector cooperation can succeed under this hexagram, but only if intentions are transparent, responsibilities are clear, and promises are realistic. In love and relationships, this is a favorable sign for heartfelt communication, repairing misunderstandings, confessing true feelings, and rebuilding trust. However, it warns against making emotional promises without corresponding action, since that damages confidence more deeply than silence. In finances, Inner Truth does not favor speculation or flashy gains. It supports steady income built through reputation, reliability, ethical dealings, and long-term partnerships. Investments should be grounded in what one truly understands rather than in seductive appearances. In health, the hexagram points to the connection between inner honesty and physical well-being. Emotional suppression, self-betrayal, and insincere living may show up as tension, poor sleep, chest tightness, digestive imbalance, or nervous strain. Honest expression, regular routine, and calming movement help restore coherence. Overall, in modern life, the message of Inner Truth is simple: trustworthiness is one of the most valuable forms of capital.
historicalStoryZh: 可借汉代季布“一诺千金”来说明中孚之义。季布为人刚毅，重然诺，民间流传“得黄金百斤，不如得季布一诺”。即便身处乱世、仕途浮沉，他最可贵之处仍在于言出必行，因此能在危难中得到他人相助。后来季布虽曾因旧事遭追捕，却因素有诚信之名，终获转圜。中孚所重，正是这种不凭权势而凭信用立身的力量。一个人若内心真诚、守信不欺，即使暂时失势，也往往有人愿意信任、扶持。反之，若徒有高言而无实德，即使一时得势，也难长久。季布之事说明，诚信不是空泛美德，而是在现实世界中能够救急、成事、聚人的真正资本。
historicalStoryEn: A fitting historical illustration is Ji Bu of the Han era, whose promise was said to be worth more than a hundred catties of gold. He was known for firmness of character and absolute reliability. In a turbulent age, with political fortunes constantly shifting, his greatest strength was not rank or wealth but the fact that his word could be trusted. Even when he later faced danger because of past associations, the respect he had earned through sincerity helped create room for rescue and reversal. This captures the spirit of Inner Truth precisely. The power of this hexagram does not come from display, force, or status, but from the moral credit built by consistent honesty. A truly trustworthy person can still gather support in hard times because others believe in that person’s core. By contrast, someone full of grand language but empty in substance may rise quickly yet cannot endure. Ji Bu’s story shows that sincerity is not merely a private virtue; it is practical capital that protects, connects, and accomplishes things in the real world.
relatedHexagramsNote: 中孚与第60卦节相邻，节讲制度分寸，中孚讲内在诚信；无节之孚易流于滥情，无孚之节则流于刻板。又与第59卦涣相通，涣是离散后重聚，中孚是重聚后以信维系。若比较第8卦比，中孚更强调关系建立的内核，不止亲附，更重真心相感。
references: 《周易·中孚卦》
《彖传》：中孚，柔在内而刚得中。说而巽，孚，乃化邦也。
《象传》：泽上有风，中孚；君子以议狱缓死。
王弼《周易注》
孔颖达《周易正义》

judgmentZh: 小过：亨，利贞，可小事，不可大事。飞鸟遗之音，不宜上宜下，大吉。
judgmentEn: Preponderance of the Small. Success. Perseverance furthers. Small things may be done; great things should not be done. The flying bird brings the message: It is not well to strive upward, it is well to remain below. Great good fortune.
imageZh: 山上有雷，小过；君子以行过乎恭，丧过乎哀，用过乎俭。
imageEn: Thunder on the mountain: the image of Preponderance of the Small. Thus in his conduct the superior man gives preponderance to reverence. In bereavement he gives preponderance to grief. In his expenditures he gives preponderance to thrift.
interpretationZh: 小过卦象征小有过越，山上有雷，雷声虽大但被山阻隔。宜做小事，不宜做大事，谦卑处下则大吉。
interpretationEn: Xiao Guo symbolizes slight excess. Thunder on the mountain is loud but contained. Attend to small matters, not great ones. Humility below brings great fortune.

lines.textZh: 初六：飞鸟以凶。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: The bird meets with misfortune through flying.
lines.interpretationZh: 飞鸟高飞而遭凶险，不宜冒进。
lines.interpretationEn: The bird flies too high and meets misfortune. Do not overreach.
lines.detailZh: 初六处卦之始，阴柔而居下，本应安分守位，却急于如飞鸟般上动，故曰“飞鸟以凶”。此爻警示：能力未备、时机未成时，最忌轻举妄动。凡事刚起步，若心先飘起，行动先拔高，往往尚未立稳根基便招来挫败。宜先察势、蓄力、守拙。
lines.detailEn: At the beginning, the yielding line is in a low place where it should remain modest and grounded. Yet it moves upward like a flying bird, hence “the bird brings misfortune.” This line warns against premature action. When ability and timing are still immature, early ambition becomes dangerous. At the start of any endeavor, one should build foundations before trying to rise or impress.

lines.textZh: 六二：过其祖，遇其妣；不及其君，遇其臣，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: She passes by her ancestor and meets her ancestress. He does not reach his prince and meets the official. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 越过祖父而遇到祖母，未及君主而遇到臣子，无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Passing the grandfather, meeting the grandmother. Not reaching the prince, meeting the minister. No blame.
lines.detailZh: 六二柔顺得中，虽有“过”而不过分，所以“无咎”。“过其祖，遇其妣；不及其君，遇其臣”，象征不走最高路线，而取次一级的合宜之道。此爻表示在礼法与层级中采取迂回、柔和、适度的处理方式，不强求正面突破，反而能避害得安。
lines.detailEn: The second yin line is central and correct in spirit, so its exceeding remains measured and blameless. “Passing the grandfather, meeting the grandmother; not reaching the ruler, meeting the minister” suggests taking an indirect but appropriate path rather than forcing the highest or most direct connection. This line favors tact, moderation, and workable intermediacy over frontal ambition.

lines.textZh: 九三：弗过防之，从或戕之，凶。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: If one is not extraordinarily careful, somebody may come from behind and strike him. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 不加倍防备，可能被人从背后伤害，凶险。
lines.interpretationEn: Without extraordinary caution, one may be struck from behind. Misfortune.
lines.detailZh: 九三以阳刚居下卦之上，易自恃其能。爻辞曰“弗过防之，从或戕之，凶”，意为若不以适度之心自我防范，反而顺着冲动、任其发展，便会遭受伤害。此爻告诫强者：真正的危险不在外敌，而在忽视戒备、放纵刚气。越有本事，越要知止。
lines.detailEn: The third line is firm and strong, positioned where self-confidence can become overconfidence. “If one does not guard against excess, one may be injured—misfortune.” The danger here is not only external attack but failure of self-restraint. A capable person who neglects caution becomes vulnerable precisely because of his own momentum. Strength now requires vigilance and limits.

lines.textZh: 九四：无咎，弗过遇之。往厉必戒，勿用永贞。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: No blame. He meets him without passing by. Going brings danger. One must be on guard. Do not act. Be constantly persevering.
lines.interpretationZh: 无过失，不越过而恰好遇到。前往有危险，必须警戒，不可妄动。
lines.interpretationEn: No blame. Meeting without overstepping. Going forward is dangerous — be cautious and do not act rashly.
lines.detailZh: 九四曰“无咎，弗过遇之。往厉必戒，勿用永贞。”此爻说明当事者若不再主动求过，只在情势中相遇应对，则可无咎；但若继续前往，则有危险，必须警惕。“勿用永贞”并非否定正道，而是提醒不可把一时之守法僵化为长期硬撑。处小过之时，应随机制宜。
lines.detailEn: The fourth line says there is no blame if one does not push beyond measure but simply meets the situation as it comes. Yet advancing further brings danger and requires warning. “Do not use enduring perseverance” means that even correctness must not harden into inflexible persistence when the time is delicate. One must remain adaptive rather than stubbornly pressing forward.

lines.textZh: 六五：密云不雨，自我西郊，公弋取彼在穴。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Dense clouds, no rain from our western territory. The prince shoots and hits him who is in the cave.
lines.interpretationZh: 密云不雨，来自西郊。公侯射箭取得穴中之物，虽有努力但时机未到。
lines.interpretationEn: Dense clouds but no rain from the west. The prince shoots into the cave. Effort is made but the time is not yet ripe.
lines.detailZh: 六五柔居尊位，有中而无猛，故见“密云不雨，自我西郊；公弋取彼在穴”。云密而未雨，象征机缘正在聚集，却未到全面发动之时；弋取在穴之物，则是从隐微、局部处获得成果。此爻利于静候时机、精准出手，不利大张旗鼓。
lines.detailEn: The fifth line occupies an honored place with yielding centrality rather than force. “Dense clouds, no rain, from our western outskirts; the prince shoots and takes what is in the cave.” Conditions are gathering, but not yet ready for broad release. Success comes through precise, limited action directed at what is hidden and near, not through expansive display or premature initiative.

lines.textZh: 上六：弗遇过之，飞鸟离之，凶，是谓灾眚。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: He passes him by, not meeting him. The flying bird leaves him. Misfortune. This means bad luck and injury.
lines.interpretationZh: 错过而不相遇，飞鸟远离，凶险，这就是灾祸。
lines.interpretationEn: Missing the encounter entirely. The bird flies away. Misfortune — this is calamity.
lines.detailZh: 上六居极而不知返，“弗遇过之，飞鸟离之，凶，是谓灾眚。”这里的“过”已变成真正的越界。既不与时相遇，又一味高飞远离，终于失其所依而致灾。此爻是全卦最重的警戒：小过之道贵在知止，若把小事之宜误作大进之机，结局往往由失误转成灾祸。
lines.detailEn: At the top, excess reaches its extreme and no longer knows how to return. “Not meeting but passing beyond; the flying bird leaves it—misfortune; this is calamity and error.” Here excess becomes true transgression. One loses contact with reality and rises too far from support. This final line gives the strongest warning of the hexagram: small adjustment is wise, but turning a limited opening into grand overreach leads to real disaster.

overviewZh: 小过卦，上震下艮，雷在山上，声势虽显，却不成大雨，象征力量有限、宜小不宜大。此卦所讲的“过”，不是鲁莽逾矩，而是在局势逼仄、能量不足时，以略微超过常规的谨慎、恭敬、节制来求通达。卦意强调可以处理细节、修补漏洞、完成局部调整，却不适合发动宏大计划、强行突破格局。飞鸟之象尤为关键：鸟鸣掠空，声音先闻而形未安，提示人在此时应当下行、收敛、务实，从低处着手，循序而进。小过不是失败之卦，而是告诉人：当时位不利、大势未成时，守分中求精，慎微中求成，反能积累转机。
overviewEn: Hexagram 62, Preponderance of the Small, is formed by Thunder above Mountain. Thunder sounds above the mountain, noticeable yet limited in force, suggesting a time when energy is real but insufficient for grand undertakings. The word “excess” here does not mean reckless overreach; it points to slight, deliberate adjustments beyond the ordinary in order to preserve order, humility, and restraint. This hexagram favors small matters, detail work, corrections, and careful accommodation, while discouraging major ventures or dramatic advances. The image of the flying bird is central: its cry is heard above, yet safety lies below. That symbolism advises lowering oneself, staying grounded, and acting through modest, practical steps rather than ambitious ascent. Xiao Guo is not a hexagram of defeat, but of proportion. When timing, position, or resources are limited, one succeeds by honoring limits, refining conduct, and letting small right actions accumulate into meaningful progress.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞言“亨，利贞，可小事，不可大事”，指出小过之时并非闭塞，而是可以通达，只是通达的方式在于守正与知分。彖传说：“小过，小者过而亨也。过以利贞，与时行也。柔得中，是以小事吉也。刚失位而不中，是以不可大事也。有飞鸟之象焉，有飞鸟遗之音，不宜上宜下，大吉；上逆而下顺也。”其义在于：阴柔居中，适合柔性处理、细部经营，故小事可成；阳刚失位，不足以担当大局，故大事难成。此时若仍以强进之心求大功，必致逆势；若能顺势下降，回到现实、基层、细节、礼法之中，反而得吉。小过的核心，不是鼓励人委屈求全，而是教人辨认时势分寸，在不宜张扬之际，以审慎之德保存元气。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says, “Success. Perseverance furthers. Small matters may be done; great matters should not be done.” This means the time of Small Preponderance is not closed or hopeless, but its success depends on correctness, proportion, and acting within scale. The Tuan Commentary explains: “In Small Preponderance, the small exceed and thereby attain success. To exceed in a way that furthers perseverance is to move with the time. The yielding obtain the center; therefore small matters are auspicious. The firm have lost their place and are not centered; therefore great matters should not be undertaken. There is the image of a flying bird. The message left by the flying bird says: it is not fitting to rise, it is fitting to descend—great good fortune. To go upward is contrary; to go downward is compliant.” The point is clear: yielding, balanced qualities are suitable now, while forceful ambition lacks proper position. Large enterprises fail because the structural conditions are not ready. Yet careful responsiveness, modest duties, and grounded conduct align with the moment. The hexagram teaches not self-diminishment for its own sake, but fidelity to timing. By accepting limits and acting accurately within them, one preserves strength and achieves real, if smaller, success.
imageDetailZh: 象辞曰“山上有雷，小过；君子以行过乎恭，丧过乎哀，用过乎俭。”山本沉静，雷主动而震，两者相遇，形成外动内止的格局：内在须守，外在可警。君子观此，不在功业上逞强，而在德行上稍加深厚。行为上更恭敬，以免轻慢；居丧时更哀敬，以存真情；用度上更节俭，以防奢溢。所谓“过乎”，并非失中，而是在易于散乱、浮扬的环境里，用略重的礼与节来校正偏差。因此小过之象，是以微调取平衡，以自敛防失度。
imageDetailEn: The Image says, “Thunder on the mountain: the image of Small Preponderance. Thus the noble one in conduct exceeds in reverence, in mourning exceeds in sorrow, and in expenditure exceeds in thrift.” Mountain is still and rooted, while thunder is sudden and stirring. Together they create a condition of outer alertness combined with inner restraint. The superior person does not respond by seeking dramatic achievement, but by deepening moral precision. In conduct, one becomes more respectful so as not to drift into carelessness. In grief, one becomes more sincerely mournful so as not to become superficial. In use of resources, one becomes more frugal so as not to slide into excess. The phrase “exceeds” does not imply imbalance for its own sake. It means applying a slightly stronger corrective where the age tends toward looseness, vanity, or impulsiveness. The image therefore teaches equilibrium through subtle self-limitation: one preserves order not by grand control, but by small, disciplined adjustments.
modernApplicationZh: 事业：小过之时，最适合优化流程、修正错误、补强细节，不宜贸然扩张、跳槽赌大势或一次性铺开过大项目。宜先把执行、协作、交付质量做到极致。感情：此卦提醒放下强烈控制欲，以体贴、倾听、守礼来修补关系。小误会可及时化解，重大承诺则应缓行，避免情绪上头。财运：利于节流、整理账目、谨慎投资，尤其适合处理零散收入、旧账、保守配置；不利高杠杆、追热点、冒险押注。健康：重点在“小病早治、作息早调”，例如睡眠、脾胃、焦虑、筋骨劳损等问题，要在轻微阶段及时干预。整体而言，小过不是停滞，而是提醒现代人：越在资源有限、局面复杂时，越要依靠微小而正确的动作累积成果。先把姿态放低、把节奏放慢、把细节做深，等时机成熟，再谋更大跨越。
modernApplicationEn: Career: this is an excellent time for process improvement, error correction, quality control, and careful execution, but not for reckless expansion, dramatic career bets, or oversized launches. Focus on tightening operations and doing small things exceptionally well. Relationships: the hexagram advises reducing control, pride, and emotional escalation. Repair bonds through listening, courtesy, and thoughtful gestures. Small misunderstandings can be resolved now, but major commitments should be approached slowly. Wealth: favorable for budgeting, clearing accounts, conservative allocation, and dealing with scattered or modest income streams. It is not favorable for leverage, trend-chasing, or speculative risk. Health: pay attention to minor imbalances before they become serious—sleep disruption, digestive weakness, anxiety, tension, or overuse injuries should be addressed early. Overall, Small Preponderance does not mean stagnation. It tells modern people that in times of limited resources or unstable conditions, progress comes through small, correct actions repeated consistently. Lower your posture, slow your tempo, deepen your care, and let disciplined modesty prepare the ground for later expansion.
historicalStoryZh: 可借周公制礼作喻。周初天下未稳，非可恣意尚武、大兴土木之时，周公反而从礼乐、丧祭、朝聘、用度等细微制度着手，谨慎厘定尊卑次序，使天下在细节中归于秩序。这正合小过之义：不以大开大阖取胜，而以小处精审积成大治。又如战国名将善守者，往往不是急于决战，而是先固营垒、修粮道、安军心，于细小处防患，故能转危为安。小过所贵，正是这种在未可大举之际，以小心、敬慎、节用与预防来护持全局的智慧。
historicalStoryEn: A fitting historical analogy is the work of the Duke of Zhou in the early Zhou dynasty. The realm was not yet stable, and it was not a time for unrestrained military ambition or extravagant construction. Instead, he devoted attention to rites, mourning, court protocol, hierarchy, and proper use of resources. Through careful ordering of what seemed small, he helped secure what was politically great. This reflects the spirit of Small Preponderance: not winning through sweeping gestures, but through disciplined refinement in particulars. Another parallel may be found in defensive generals who avoided premature decisive battle. They first strengthened camps, secured supply lines, and stabilized morale. By preventing danger in minor places, they preserved the larger whole. The wisdom of this hexagram lies precisely there: when a great move is untimely, preserve the situation through vigilance, restraint, and careful management of small things.
relatedHexagramsNote: 小过与大过相对：大过讲栋梁将倾，需非常之举；小过则讲时位未足，只宜微调，不宜强撑。它也近于谦卦之意，都重收敛自下，但小过更强调“当下只能做小事”的时势判断。
references: 《周易·小过卦》
《彖传》：小过，小者过而亨也……上逆而下顺也。
《象传》：山上有雷，小过；君子以行过乎恭，丧过乎哀，用过乎俭。
王弼《周易注》
程颐《伊川易传》
朱熹《周易本义》

judgmentZh: 既济：亨小，利贞，初吉终乱。
judgmentEn: After Completion. Success in small matters. Perseverance furthers. At the beginning good fortune, at the end disorder.
imageZh: 水在火上，既济；君子以思患而预防之。
imageEn: Water over fire: the image of the condition in After Completion. Thus the superior man takes thought of misfortune and arms himself against it in advance.
interpretationZh: 既济卦象征事已成就，水在火上，阴阳各得其位。虽已完成但须警惕，初始吉利而终将生乱。
interpretationEn: Ji Ji symbolizes completion. Water over fire — all lines are in their proper places. Though complete, one must remain vigilant. Good fortune at first, disorder at the end.

lines.textZh: 初九：曳其轮，濡其尾，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine at the beginning: He brakes his wheels. He gets his tail in the water. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 拖住车轮减速，尾巴沾湿了水，谨慎行事则无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Braking the wheels, the tail gets wet. Caution at the start brings no blame.
lines.detailZh: “曳其轮，濡其尾，无咎。”初九处既济之始，虽有行动之志，却先见险情，因此拖住车轮、放缓前进，甚至尾部沾水，也不贸然冒进。此爻强调在完成初期不可因顺利而失谨慎，宁可慢一点、湿一点，也比仓促失控更好。其义在于节制起步、预防小失，故最终无咎。
lines.detailEn: “He drags back his wheels and gets his tail wet. No blame.” At the beginning of completion, there is momentum to move ahead, yet danger is already visible. The image of restraining the wheels suggests deliberate caution and controlled pace. Even if one is slightly inconvenienced or delayed, that is preferable to rushing into instability. This line teaches disciplined restraint at the start of apparent success; careful slowing prevents larger fault, hence no blame.

lines.textZh: 六二：妇丧其茀，勿逐，七日得。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: The woman loses the curtain of her carriage. Do not run after it; on the seventh day you will get it.
lines.interpretationZh: 妇人丢失了车帘，不必追赶，七日后自然失而复得。
lines.interpretationEn: The woman loses her carriage curtain. Do not chase it; in seven days it returns.
lines.detailZh: “妇丧其茀，勿逐，七日得。”六二柔顺中正，象征在秩序中一时有所失落，如妇人失去车饰与遮蔽物。爻意不是教人焦躁追索，而是守中等待，因其本不真正失道，时机一转，自会复得。“七日”有循环一周、往复归正之意。此爻告诉人，在稳定局面中出现暂时缺失，不必过度反应，沉着比追赶更重要。
lines.detailEn: “The woman loses her curtain. Do not chase it; after seven days she gets it back.” This line concerns a temporary loss within an otherwise ordered situation. The proper response is not anxious pursuit but calm trust in timing. Because the center is retained, what is lost can return naturally. “Seven days” suggests a full cycle of return. The lesson is that not every absence requires frantic recovery; in stable conditions, patience and inner steadiness often restore what haste would disturb.

lines.textZh: 九三：高宗伐鬼方，三年克之，小人勿用。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The Illustrious Ancestor disciplines the Devil's Country. After three years he conquers it. Inferior people must not be employed.
lines.interpretationZh: 高宗讨伐鬼方，三年才征服。大事需要时间，不可用小人。
lines.interpretationEn: The Illustrious Ancestor conquers the Devil's Country after three years. Do not employ inferior people.
lines.detailZh: “高宗伐鬼方，三年克之，小人勿用。”九三居下卦之终，刚健而临险，意味着真正的难关仍需长期攻坚。高宗三年而克，说明大事虽成，靠的是持续毅力与正确用人，而不是一时锋芒。后句“小人勿用”尤为关键：事业接近完成时，最忌让短视、贪功、德薄之人介入中枢，否则胜局也会生乱。
lines.detailEn: “Gaozong attacked the Demon Region and after three years conquered it. Petty people must not be employed.” This line shows that even in a completed situation, major challenges still demand endurance. Victory takes years, not impulse. The warning against petty people is central: after hard-won progress, shallow, opportunistic, or self-serving actors can undo the whole achievement. The line advises perseverance, strategic patience, and strict judgment in choosing allies.

lines.textZh: 六四：繻有衣袽，终日戒。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The finest clothes turn to rags. Be careful all day long.
lines.interpretationZh: 华丽的衣服下备有破布，整日保持警戒。
lines.interpretationEn: Fine garments may become rags. Stay vigilant throughout the day.
lines.detailZh: “繻有衣袽，终日戒。”六四已入上卦，接近权位与成局中心，看似安定，实则更须防患。华美的衣饰之下要预备破布补缀，象征在体面、丰足之外保留应急方案。所谓“终日戒”，不是恐惧，而是全天候的清醒管理。此爻强调守成阶段要有备份意识、补漏意识与危机预案。
lines.detailEn: “There are fine garments, and also rags beneath them. Be on guard all day.” Entering the upper trigram, one is close to the center of established order. Outwardly things look polished, yet preparedness remains necessary. Fine clothing with rags ready underneath symbolizes the wisdom of maintaining contingency beneath appearance and success. To be vigilant all day means continuous alertness, not panic. This line teaches backup planning, maintenance, and humble readiness.

lines.textZh: 九五：东邻杀牛，不如西邻之禴祭，实受其福。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: The neighbor in the east who slaughters an ox does not attain as much real happiness as the neighbor in the west with his small offering.
lines.interpretationZh: 东邻杀牛大祭，不如西邻简朴之祭实际受福。诚心胜过排场。
lines.interpretationEn: The eastern neighbor's grand sacrifice gains less than the western neighbor's simple offering. Sincerity surpasses display.
lines.detailZh: “东邻杀牛，不如西邻之禴祭，实受其福。”九五居尊位而得中，指出既济之时，形式上的盛大不如诚敬合度。东邻杀牛，排场隆重；西邻薄祭，反而因真诚而得福。此爻批评成事之后以奢侈、夸功、表演来维持秩序的做法，主张回到真实、节制与内在诚意。守成最靠德，不靠声势。
lines.detailEn: “The eastern neighbor slaughters an ox; it is not equal to the western neighbor’s simple seasonal offering, which truly receives blessing.” In the ruler’s place, this line distinguishes genuine sincerity from grand display. Lavish sacrifice does not surpass modest but reverent offering. After success, people often rely on spectacle, ceremony, or self-congratulation. This line rejects that tendency. What preserves blessing is authenticity, proportion, and inner integrity, not outward extravagance.

lines.textZh: 上六：濡其首，厉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: He gets his head in the water. Danger.
lines.interpretationZh: 头浸入水中，危险。过度冒进，陷入险境。
lines.interpretationEn: The head gets into the water. Danger from going too far.
lines.detailZh: “濡其首，厉。”上六居既济之极，完成到了尽头，反而最危险。前爻尚只是“濡尾”，此处却“濡首”，表示涉水过深、失其节度，已经逼近倾覆。其象说明成局若不知止，不知退，不知再调，便会从顶点滑向危殆。此爻是全卦终极警告：成功不可迷，完成不可满，过度自信最易招致险厉。
lines.detailEn: “He gets his head wet. Danger.” At the top of Ji Ji, completion has reached its extreme and therefore turns precarious. Earlier the tail was wet; now the whole head is immersed, indicating overextension and loss of proper measure. This is the climax of the hexagram’s warning: when one pushes too far in a situation already brought to completion, danger follows quickly. Success must know limits. Without timely withdrawal, rebalancing, or humility, the summit becomes the threshold of decline.

overviewZh: 既济卦，上坎下离，水在火上，象征事物已经调和成形、阶段性完成，故名“既济”。此卦所写并非艰难开创，而是成功之后的守成难题。卦辞言“亨小，利贞；初吉终乱”，说明局面起初顺利，秩序分明，阴阳各得其位，看似圆满；但越是在完成之后，越容易因自满、松懈、轻敌而走向混乱。既济真正的智慧，不在庆功，而在戒惧；不在扩张，而在维持。它提醒人：天下没有绝对稳固的完成态，任何“已成”都潜伏着“将变”，任何胜局都需要通过谨慎、节制、预防与持续修整来延续。对个人而言，此卦尤其适用于项目收尾、关系稳定、事业成熟之际，告诫人在最顺的时候，正要生出最深的警觉心。
overviewEn: Hexagram 63, Jì Jì (Already Fording / After Completion), consists of Water above Fire. It depicts a condition in which opposing forces have been properly arranged and a difficult passage has, at least outwardly, been completed. Yet this hexagram is not a simple celebration of success. Its core warning is that completion is never final. The Judgment says, “Small prosperity; perseverance is beneficial. At first good fortune, in the end disorder.” The structure appears perfect because yin and yang occupy their proper places, but this very balance is fragile. Once people feel a task is done, they become careless, complacent, or overly confident. Thus the true lesson of Ji Ji is not how to begin, but how to preserve what has been achieved. It teaches vigilance after success, humility in stable times, and preventive thinking before trouble emerges. In practical life, it applies to mature careers, settled relationships, and projects near completion: the moment everything seems secure is precisely when one must become more disciplined, attentive, and self-correcting.
judgmentDetailZh: 《彖传》说：“既济，亨，小者亨也。利贞，刚柔正而位当也。初吉，柔得中也。终止则乱，其道穷也。”这段话点明了既济卦的结构之美与命运之险。所谓“亨小”，不是大开大合的盛运，而是在既有秩序中的细密通达；因为六爻阴阳相应、刚柔得位，所以局面一时安稳，可守可成。所谓“利贞”，强调越是成局，越不能失其正道，必须守原则、守分寸、守节奏。初时之吉，在于柔顺得中、行动合宜，并非靠强攻得胜；但“终止则乱”，意味着若把完成当作终点，停止警醒与调整，则生机转为僵化，秩序反而从内部松解。既济之道，是在成功中继续修德，在安定中预思危险，在有序中留出调节空间。它不是鼓励悲观，而是要求成熟：真正有智慧的人，不会被眼前的圆满迷住，而会看见圆满之后最容易出现的裂缝。
judgmentDetailEn: The Tuan Commentary explains: “Ji Ji means success; the lesser is successful. Beneficial is perseverance, because the firm and yielding are correct and their places appropriate. Initial good fortune comes because the yielding obtains the center. If one comes to a halt at the end, disorder arises; the way reaches its limit.” This commentary reveals both the beauty and danger of the hexagram. “Small success” does not mean a grand, expansive triumph, but orderly progress within a properly arranged structure. Because the lines are correctly positioned, the situation is temporarily balanced and workable. Yet this balance survives only through perseverance. “Beneficial is correctness” means that after completion one must hold to principle, proportion, and discipline even more carefully than before. The initial good fortune comes not from aggression, but from centered and fitting conduct. The warning that “if one stops at the end, disorder arises” is crucial: the moment one treats completion as final and ceases adjustment, decline begins. Ji Ji therefore teaches post-success maturity. One must continue cultivating character after achievement, foresee danger during peace, and keep flexibility within order. It is not pessimistic; it is realistic. Wisdom lies in seeing that apparent perfection contains the seeds of disruption unless consciously maintained.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》曰：“水在火上，既济；君子以思患而预防之。”火本炎上，水本润下，如今水在火上，二者既相制又相成，形成暂时平衡，这正是“既济”的图景。其妙在于表面和谐，内里却始终存在张力：水若过盛则灭火，火若过炽则耗水，平衡稍失，完成即转为失序。因此君子观此象，不会沉醉于已成之功，而是居安思危，预先堵塞漏洞，提早设置缓冲与防线。既济不是静止的安稳，而是动态维持的安稳。一个人若能在事情顺利时就检查细节、修补隐患、节制欲望，便能延长成果；若只顾享受成就，不问风险，便容易在转瞬之间由吉入乱。此象强调的，是成熟治理中的预警意识。
imageDetailEn: The Image Commentary says, “Water above fire: Already Completed. Thus the noble person thinks about misfortune and prepares against it in advance.” Fire naturally rises and water naturally descends, yet here water stands above fire. This arrangement suggests a rare and temporary balance in which opposing forces restrain and support one another at once. That is the image of completion. But its harmony is never inert. If water overwhelms, the fire is extinguished; if fire flares too strongly, the balance fails. So the noble person does not merely admire the completed state. Instead, they anticipate what could go wrong and prepare before trouble appears. Ji Ji is not static stability but stability maintained through awareness. In practical terms, this means inspecting details while things go well, repairing hidden weaknesses before they widen, and restraining excess even during prosperity. Success without vigilance decays quickly. The image therefore emphasizes preventive intelligence: the higher form of wisdom is not emergency rescue after collapse, but foresight that keeps collapse from happening at all.
modernApplicationZh: 在现代应用上，既济卦最适合解释“已经做成之后怎么办”。事业上，它表示项目进入交付、团队进入成熟、公司进入稳定阶段，此时不宜盲目冒进，也不宜因成绩而松弛管理，重点应放在流程复盘、风险控制、制度补漏与人才节制使用，尤其警惕“小人勿用”所指的短视与邀功之人。感情上，既济常见于关系已确立、婚姻已成、相处趋稳之时，问题不在有没有感情，而在是否因熟悉而失去珍惜，需要通过沟通、边界与日常经营来防止热情冷却。财运上，象征已有收获、现金流尚可，但不可激进扩张或轻信高收益，应重视资产配置、储备金与风险对冲，守成为上。健康上，多提示表面稳定而暗藏隐忧，尤其要注意作息紊乱、慢性疲劳、心肾失衡及情绪紧绷，宜在无病时先调养。总体而言，既济不是“万事大吉”，而是“成后更慎”。
modernApplicationEn: In modern life, Hexagram 63 addresses the question: what should one do after things have already succeeded? In career matters, it often appears when a project is nearing delivery, a team has become mature, or a business has entered a stable phase. This is not the time for reckless expansion, nor for relaxing management because results look good. The priority should be process review, risk control, patching structural weaknesses, and being selective about who is entrusted with influence—especially in light of the warning that petty or shortsighted people should not be used. In relationships, Ji Ji often refers to a bond that is already established: a marriage, a long partnership, or a stable emotional pattern. The risk lies not in lack of affection but in taking closeness for granted; care, communication, and daily maintenance become essential. Financially, it suggests existing gains and workable cash flow, yet warns against aggressive speculation or overconfidence. Reserve funds, balanced allocation, and prudent defense matter more than chasing upside. In health, it often points to an apparently stable condition masking deeper imbalance, such as chronic fatigue, stress, disrupted routines, or system-level depletion. Overall, this hexagram does not mean “all is well forever,” but rather “success must now be guarded with greater care.”
historicalStoryZh: 既济卦可联系卦中“高宗伐鬼方，三年克之”来理解。高宗一般被认为是商王武丁，他中兴殷商，整顿朝政，对外用兵，历经多年艰苦方有成效。此事正能说明既济之理：真正的完成，不是轻易得来，而是通过长期整合、反复经营才达到阶段性安定。即便胜利已经出现，也不可因此轻启骄心，因为大战之后尚有抚定、修养、分配、守成等更复杂的问题。若误以为攻克即大功告成，后患便会滋生。故爻辞又特别说“小人勿用”，提醒治世之后最怕用人失当、赏罚失衡。历史上的中兴之君，多亡于后继轻忽；而能久安者，往往是胜后尤谨、治后尤戒。既济因此不仅讲成功，更讲成功之后的政治与人事智慧。
historicalStoryEn: A classic historical illustration of Hexagram 63 appears in the line, “Gaozong attacked the Demon Region and after three years conquered it.” Gaozong is commonly identified with King Wu Ding of the Shang dynasty, a ruler associated with political renewal and disciplined governance. The story embodies the logic of Ji Ji: real completion is not easy, quick, or casual. It is achieved through prolonged effort, strategic patience, and repeated correction until a workable order finally emerges. Yet even after victory, the work is not over. Stabilization, reconstruction, fair reward, and careful governance all become crucial. If one mistakes military success—or any breakthrough—for final completion, new dangers immediately begin to grow. That is why the line adds the warning, “Do not employ petty people.” After success, poor appointments and shallow opportunists can unravel what hard effort built. History repeatedly shows that renewal is often lost not during struggle, but after triumph, when later actors become complacent. Ji Ji thus speaks not only of success itself, but of the statecraft and human judgment required to preserve success.
relatedHexagramsNote: 既济与第64卦未济互为综卦，一言“已成”，一言“未成”。既济重在成后防乱，未济重在未成慎进。二者合观，可知《易》不把完成看作终点，而把完成与未完成视为循环往复的过程：成中有危，未成中有机。
references: 《周易·既济卦》
《彖传》：既济，亨，小者亨也。利贞，刚柔正而位当也。初吉，柔得中也。终止则乱，其道穷也。
《象传》：水在火上，既济；君子以思患而预防之。
王弼《周易注》
孔颖达《周易正义》

judgmentZh: 未济：亨，小狐汔济，濡其尾，无攸利。
judgmentEn: Before Completion. Success. But if the little fox, after nearly completing the crossing, gets his tail in the water, there is nothing that would further.
imageZh: 火在水上，未济；君子以慎辨物居方。
imageEn: Fire over water: the image of the condition before transition. Thus the superior man is careful in the differentiation of things, so that each finds its place.
interpretationZh: 未济卦象征事未完成，火在水上，阴阳不交。如小狐渡河将济而未济，须谨慎辨别事物各安其位。
interpretationEn: Wei Ji symbolizes the state before completion. Fire over water — they do not interact. Like a young fox nearly across the stream, caution is needed to place everything properly.

lines.textZh: 初六：濡其尾，吝。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: He gets his tail in the water. Humiliating.
lines.interpretationZh: 尾巴浸入水中，有所困窘。冒进而力不足。
lines.interpretationEn: The tail gets in the water. Humiliation from overreaching without sufficient strength.
lines.detailZh: “濡其尾，吝。”初六居未济之始，才弱而处险，像渡河者一开始就弄湿尾巴，行动未稳便先受牵累。此爻告诫人在开局阶段不要急进，尤其能力、资源、判断尚不足时，越想抢先越易失措。所谓“吝”，并非大凶，而是小处失当、自取困窘；若能及早收敛，仍可避免更大失误。
lines.detailEn: “He wets his tail. Humiliation.” The first line stands at the beginning of an unfinished crossing. It is weak and exposed to danger, like someone who loses balance before truly setting out. The warning is against haste when one’s strength, preparation, or judgment is still insufficient. The disgrace here is not total ruin but an avoidable embarrassment caused by premature movement. If one restrains oneself early, greater trouble can still be prevented.

lines.textZh: 九二：曳其轮，贞吉。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: He brakes his wheels. Perseverance brings good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 拖住车轮不急于前进，坚守正道则吉。
lines.interpretationEn: Braking the wheels. Patience and perseverance bring good fortune.
lines.detailZh: “曳其轮，贞吉。”九二以刚居中，虽有行动能力，却懂得暂缓前进，如拉住车轮，不使疾驰失控。未济之时最贵这种节制：不是不能进，而是知道何时先稳住。守正则吉，说明在局势未定时，中道与自持胜过逞强。此爻适合解释为战略性停顿，为的是等待条件成熟，再求安全渡过。
lines.detailEn: “He drags his wheels. Perseverance brings good fortune.” Firm yet centered, the second line has the power to move, but chooses restraint. Dragging the wheels suggests slowing the vehicle so it does not rush into danger. In a time before completion, this kind of disciplined pause is highly valuable. It is not passivity, but controlled timing. Good fortune comes from staying correct and refusing to let impatience outrun reality.

lines.textZh: 六三：未济，征凶，利涉大川。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Before completion, attack brings misfortune. It furthers one to cross the great water.
lines.interpretationZh: 事未完成就出征则凶，但利于涉越大川，需选择正确方向。
lines.interpretationEn: Before completion, attacking brings misfortune. But crossing the great water furthers.
lines.detailZh: “未济，征凶，利涉大川。”六三阴柔不中，又处上下转换的关键位，若贸然前往，则有凶险；但“利涉大川”又说明不能困守原地，必须借助更大决心与正确方法完成突破。看似矛盾，实则是在说：盲目前进必凶，有准备地面对大挑战反而有利。此爻强调转折期的抉择，需要胆识，更需要方法与助力。
lines.detailEn: “Before Completion. To advance brings misfortune. It is favorable to cross the great river.” The third line is weak and improperly placed at a critical turning point. A direct push forward invites danger, yet remaining stuck is also not the answer. The seeming paradox means this: reckless advance is disastrous, but a well-prepared commitment to a major crossing can be favorable. Real transition demands both courage and proper method.

lines.textZh: 九四：贞吉，悔亡，震用伐鬼方，三年有赏于大国。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse disappears. Shock, thus to discipline the Devil's Country. For three years, great realms are rewarded.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，悔恨消失。奋力讨伐鬼方，三年后获大国封赏。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune and remorse vanishes. After three years of campaigning, great rewards come.
lines.detailZh: “贞吉，悔亡，震用伐鬼方，三年有赏于大国。”九四进入上卦，已接近成事阶段，以阳刚之才承担攻坚之任。守正则吉，过去的懊悔也可消除；但建立新秩序不是一蹴而就，像征伐鬼方一样，需要震动奋发与长期经营，历经三年方受大国之赏。此爻表明，真正的突破往往伴随艰苦持久的行动，急功近利反而不能成。
lines.detailEn: “Perseverance brings good fortune. Regret disappears. With stirring energy he attacks the Demon Country; after three years he is rewarded by the great state.” The fourth line has entered the upper trigram and nears effective action. Its strength can remove earlier regrets, but only through sustained and rightful effort. The campaign against the ‘Demon Country’ symbolizes difficult work that demands force, endurance, and time. Reward comes only after prolonged commitment, not instant triumph.

lines.textZh: 六五：贞吉，无悔，君子之光，有孚，吉。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. No remorse. The light of the superior man is true. Good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，无悔恨。君子的光辉出于诚信，吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune without remorse. The superior man's radiance shines with sincerity. Good fortune.
lines.detailZh: “贞吉，无悔，君子之光，有孚，吉。”六五为全卦主爻，以柔居尊而得中，是未济中最可成之处。其“光”不是炫耀，而是德性与判断自然显明；“有孚”则指诚信能感人、能聚众。故此爻说明，在混乱未定之际，真正能完成局面的人，未必最强硬，却一定中正、可信、能照亮方向。守此则吉，且无后悔。
lines.detailEn: “Perseverance brings good fortune. No regret. The light of the noble one. There is trust. Good fortune.” The fifth line is the ruler of the hexagram. Though yielding, it is central and properly balanced, making it the clearest point of possible success. Its ‘light’ is not display, but moral clarity and steady influence. Trust allows others to align around it. In unfinished times, the one who completes the work is not always the harshest, but the most centered, credible, and illuminating.

lines.textZh: 上九：有孚于饮酒，无咎，濡其首，有孚失是。
lines.textEn: Nine at the top: There is drinking of wine in genuine confidence. No blame. But if one wets his head, he loses it in truth.
lines.interpretationZh: 以诚信之心饮酒庆贺，无过失。但若沉溺过度，头浸入水中，则失去诚信。
lines.interpretationEn: Drinking wine with genuine confidence — no blame. But excess leads to losing one's truth.
lines.detailZh: “有孚于饮酒，无咎，濡其首，有孚失是。”上九居终极之位，事情似乎已经渡过，故可安然饮酒庆功；若真有诚信自守，本无咎害。但问题在于过度松弛与沉醉，乃至“濡其首”，连头都湿了，比初六更深陷其中。此爻警告：功成在望时最易失节，若把真诚误用为放任，把庆贺变成失度，则先前建立的一切也可能受损。
lines.detailEn: “There is trust in drinking wine. No blame. If he wets his head, he loses the truth of that trust.” At the top, the crossing seems essentially done, so celebration appears justified. If one truly remains sincere and measured, there is no blame. Yet the danger is excess: relaxation turns into indulgence, and instead of merely wetting the tail, one wets the head. This final line warns that the greatest risk may come after success seems certain. Trust must not decay into carelessness.

overviewZh: 未济是《易经》六十四卦的终卦，上离下坎，火在上、水在下，二气不交，象征事情已近完成却尚未完成，最忌在最后关头松懈。卦名“未济”，不是全盘失败，而是提醒人处在过渡、收束、再平衡的阶段：局势已有通达之机，但秩序尚未真正建立，因此一切看似接近成功，实则仍潜伏反复。此卦最有价值的启示，在于告诉人“终点之前仍是起点”，完成不是自然发生，而要靠谨慎、分辨、守正与耐心。未济居于全书之末，也象征宇宙与人生永远在流动中，没有永恒静止的圆满，真正成熟的人，不是在将成之际自满，而是在未成之时稳住心性，补齐细节，顺势而进，才能把险局化为通途。
overviewEn: Hexagram 64, Before Completion, is the final hexagram of the Yijing. With Fire above and Water below, the two forces do not yet fully unite, symbolizing a situation that is close to fulfillment but not truly finished. The name does not simply mean failure; rather, it points to a transitional stage in which movement is possible, yet order has not been secured. The central warning is about the last stretch: people often stumble not at the beginning, but when success seems near and vigilance fades. This hexagram teaches that completion requires disciplined attention, clear judgment, and patient self-command. Its placement at the end of the classic is meaningful: endings are also beginnings, and life never rests in final perfection. Mature action lies in staying steady amid uncertainty, correcting small errors before they grow, and crossing the final distance with humility. In that way, an unfinished situation can ripen into real achievement.
judgmentDetailZh: 卦辞说“未济：亨，小狐汔济，濡其尾，无攸利”，意思是局势本有可通之机，但若在临门一脚时失去谨慎，便会前功尽弃。小狐过河，已近彼岸，却因尾巴沾湿而坏了全局，正是对“差最后一步”的警示。《彖传》说：“未济，亨；柔得中也。小狐汔济，未出中也。濡其尾，无攸利；不续终也。虽不当位，刚柔应也。”其意在于，未济并非绝对不通，六五柔中得位，所以仍有亨通基础；但过程尚未走完，若不能善终，便难有利益。全卦多爻失位，说明秩序尚未稳定，不过刚柔相应，也表明仍具调整、协同与转化的可能。故此卦重心不在冒进，而在守中、慎终、修正次序，让未定之局逐渐成形。
judgmentDetailEn: The Judgment says, “Before Completion: success. The little fox, nearly across, wets its tail. Nothing is favorable.” The image is subtle: the crossing is almost done, yet the smallest lapse at the final moment ruins the effort. It is a warning about premature confidence. The Commentary on the Decision explains: “Before Completion. Success, because the soft obtains the center. The little fox nearly crosses, but has not yet left the middle. It wets its tail; nothing is favorable, because it does not carry through to the end. Though the places are not proper, the hard and soft correspond.” This means the situation is not hopeless; there is genuine potential for success because central balance remains possible, especially through the yielding but centered quality of the fifth line. Yet the process is incomplete, and what matters most is not beginning well but finishing well. Many lines are out of place, showing instability and unfinished order, but the mutual responsiveness of firm and yielding forces means correction is still possible. The hexagram therefore counsels restraint, alignment, and careful completion rather than reckless advance.
imageDetailZh: 《象传》说：“火在水上，未济；君子以慎辨物居方。”火性炎上，水性润下，火在水上，彼此不相融成用，象征万事尚未调和，结构仍待安顿。此象不是纯粹混乱，而是一种有待整理的分离状态：元素都在，却还没放到正确位置。君子观此，知道关键不只是努力，而是“慎辨物居方”——仔细分辨事物性质，使其各安其位。治理局面、经营关系、安排资源，皆需先分清轻重缓急、内外先后。未济的图像告诉人，局面越接近完成，越要重视秩序与定位；只要错置一环，整体就难收功。真正的智慧，是在纷乱未定时保持清明，让每一件事回到合宜的位置。
imageDetailEn: The Image says, “Fire above water: Before Completion. Thus the noble one carefully distinguishes things and places them in their proper positions.” Fire rises; water sinks. Though both are present, they do not yet combine into a stable working order. This does not describe total chaos, but a state in which the elements exist without full integration. The lesson is practical: success depends not merely on effort, but on right arrangement. To “distinguish things and place them properly” means recognizing the nature of each factor, assigning roles clearly, and restoring proportion where confusion remains. In leadership, relationships, and planning, the issue is often not lack of resources but misplacement, bad timing, or blurred priorities. Before completion, one must resist the temptation to celebrate too early and instead refine structure. The image teaches that clarity, classification, and proper positioning turn a nearly formed situation into one that can actually endure.
modernApplicationZh: 事业上，未济提示项目进入收尾或转型阶段时，风险常出在细节、流程与协作断点，适合复盘、补漏、校准节奏，不宜因一时顺利而贸然扩张。感情上，此卦表示关系尚在磨合，彼此有吸引也有误差，关键在坦诚沟通、厘清边界、处理旧问题，不能只靠情绪推进，否则容易在快要稳定时反复。财运上，未济不利投机式冲刺，尤其临近见利之际更应谨慎，适合保守理财、分散风险、先确认现金流与规则，再谈放大收益。健康上，多指身体处于恢复期、亚健康调整期或治疗后巩固期，看似好转却未彻底稳固，必须规律作息、持续观察、按计划修复。总体而言，未济不是坏卦，而是提醒现代人：事情最怕“差不多”，真正的成就来自最后阶段的自律、分辨与善终能力。
modernApplicationEn: In career matters, Before Completion suggests a project, partnership, or transition is in its final yet unstable phase. The main risks lie in details, handoffs, unclear responsibilities, and overconfidence. It favors review, correction, and disciplined execution rather than aggressive expansion. In relationships, the hexagram points to a bond that has real potential but still requires adjustment. Attraction may be present, yet misunderstandings or unresolved history can create reversals. Honest communication and clearer boundaries are more useful than emotional rushing. Financially, it warns against speculative pushes, especially when profit seems close at hand. This is a time for conservative planning, risk distribution, and verifying assumptions before scaling up. In health, the image often fits recovery, rehabilitation, or a fragile improvement that should not be mistaken for full stability. Consistent rest, treatment, and observation matter more than dramatic action. Overall, the hexagram is not pessimistic: it teaches modern people that “almost done” is a critical stage, and that lasting success depends on finishing with patience, structure, and self-restraint.
historicalStoryZh: 可以把未济联想到商周之际的改朝换代。周人伐纣之前，天下局势已显倾覆之势，但真正完成天命更替，并非一战即可定局。武王克商之后，还有殷民安置、旧势力平定、礼乐制度重建等长期工程。若只以牧野一胜自满，新的秩序未必能立。未济之义，正像这种“大战已过，天下未安”的时刻：表面上成功在望，实际上最考验统治者是否能慎终如始、辨别轻重、安排名分。故而未济并不是弱势之象，而是提醒人真正困难常在胜利之后。能否把破局转为定局，取决于后续的节制、诚信与秩序建构能力。
historicalStoryEn: A useful historical parallel for Before Completion is the transition from Shang to Zhou. Before the fall of King Zhou of Shang, the old order was already weakening, yet the transfer of Heaven’s Mandate could not be secured by one battle alone. Even after Wu of Zhou defeated Shang at Muye, the realm still required pacification, the former population had to be settled, and new ritual and political institutions had to be established. If the victors had treated the battlefield success as final completion, the new order might have collapsed. This is the spirit of the hexagram: the outer breakthrough may have occurred, but inner consolidation is unfinished. The hardest work often begins after apparent victory. Before Completion reminds rulers and ordinary people alike that lasting success depends less on dramatic conquest than on disciplined follow-through, trustworthiness, and the patient building of order.
relatedHexagramsNote: 未济与第63卦既济互为综卦。既济言“已成之后如何防乱”，未济言“未成之时如何成事”。二者一成一未成，揭示《易》理并无绝对终局：既成中藏未成，未成中孕育可成。读未济，宜连参既济，更能理解慎终如始的深意。
